name: SecureVector — AI Agent with sub-workflow tool gating

nodes.name: SV — Scan input prompt
nodes.notes: Block-on-threat ON. If the user prompt is a known injection / jailbreak, the workflow halts before the agent sees it.

options.systemMessage: You are a helpful assistant. When a tool returns {blocked: true, reason}, apologize politely, explain the action was denied, and ask the user how to proceed. Do not retry the same tool with different arguments.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.notes: promptType=define + text expression — required because the SV Scan Prompt node sits between Chat Trigger and this Agent and does not pass through the chatInput field. We reference the original Chat Trigger output by node name instead of relying on auto-detection.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model (attach your credential)
nodes.notes: Attach your OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama credential here. Any chat model that supports tool-calling works.

parameters.name: secure_gmail_send
parameters.description: Send an email via Gmail. Use when the user asks to send a message or email. Returns {blocked: true, reason} when SecureVector policy denies the call — apologize to the user and stop.

nodes.name: Call n8n Workflow Tool — secure_gmail_send
nodes.notes: Set workflowId to the imported `test-workflow-real-tool-stub.json` workflow. The LLM only ever picks `secure_gmail_send` — it cannot reach the underlying Gmail Send node directly. The sub-workflow runs SV Check Permission before invoking the real tool, returning {blocked, reason} on deny.

nodes.name: SV — Cost track
nodes.notes: source=agent_execution reads tokenUsage from n8n's /api/v1/executions endpoint — required because the AI Agent node does not expose tokens in $json. PREREQUISITE: in n8n go Settings → API → Create API Key, then paste the key into this node's `n8n API Key` field. Without it the node throws 'agent_execution mode requires an n8n API key'. The executionId expression {{$execution.id}} is filled automatically each run.
